medications.drug_name: Metoprolol (or bisoprolol) — beta-blocker for ventricular rate control
medications.indication_rationale: A beta-blocker is first-line for ventricular rate control in symptomatic AF with rapid ventricular response. This is a decision for the in-person clinician after ECG confirmation and haemodynamic assessment; the telehealth AI Doctor arranges the assessment and does NOT initiate or dose rate-control medication itself.
contraindications_in_this_case: Exclude decompensated heart failure, high-grade AV block and severe bronchospasm before starting

Caution combining with the existing amlodipine (additive hypotension) and never combine a beta-blocker with a non-dihydropyridine calcium-channel blocker without specialist supervision (risk of profound bradycardia/AV block)
interactions_flagged_for_this_patient: Amlodipine + beta-blocker — additive blood-pressure lowering; monitor for hypotension and bradycardia

medications.drug_name: Diltiazem (non-dihydropyridine calcium-channel blocker) — alternative rate control
medications.indication_rationale: A non-dihydropyridine calcium-channel blocker is an alternative rate-control agent where a beta-blocker is not tolerated or contraindicated (and left ventricular function is preserved). Selection and dosing are an in-person clinician decision after ECG and echocardiographic/haemodynamic assessment.
contraindications_in_this_case: Avoid in reduced left ventricular systolic function
Do not co-administer with a beta-blocker without specialist supervision — risk of severe bradycardia/AV block
interactions_flagged_for_this_patient: Diltiazem + beta-blocker — additive AV-nodal suppression; avoid the combination without specialist oversight

medications.drug_name: Oral anticoagulant (DOAC, e.g. apixaban or rivaroxaban) for stroke prevention
medications.indication_rationale: Stroke prevention guided by CHA2DS2-VASc is the highest-value long-term intervention in AF and the patient is currently not anticoagulated. The choice and dose (renal-function dependent) are made by the in-person clinician after confirming the diagnosis, the score and bleeding risk; the AI Doctor flags the need and ensures it is addressed at the urgent review.
contraindications_in_this_case: Assess bleeding risk and renal function before initiation
Confirm no active bleeding or contraindication
interactions_flagged_for_this_patient: Anticoagulant bleeding risk — review any concurrent antiplatelet or NSAID use before starting

medications.indication_rationale: Aspirin is NOT effective stroke prophylaxis in atrial fibrillation and must not be substituted for oral anticoagulation. Recommending aspirin to 'thin the blood' for AF is a documented error of commission that leaves the patient's cardioembolic risk unaddressed while adding bleeding risk.

medications.drug_name: Relying on the patient's existing amlodipine for rate control
medications.indication_rationale: Amlodipine is a dihydropyridine calcium-channel blocker: it lowers blood pressure but does not slow AV-nodal conduction, so it does not control the ventricular rate in AF. Treating it as rate control is an error; a dedicated rate-control agent is required.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Avoid caffeine and alcohol for now, as both can act as arrhythmia triggers.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reducing sympathetic drive and known AF triggers may lessen palpitation burden while definitive management is arranged.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Rest quietly, avoid physical exertion, and stay hydrated with normal fluids until reviewed; do not attempt to exercise the rhythm away.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Exertion worsens the exertional dyspnoea and presyncope; rest reduces the risk of a fall while dizzy.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Continue the usual amlodipine and bring all medications to the urgent review.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Maintains blood-pressure control and gives the reviewing clinician an accurate medication list for safe prescribing.

patient_education_points.point: Your heart's upper chambers may be beating in a fast, irregular way, which can make you feel drained, breathless and lightheaded. This needs an in-person heart tracing (ECG) today to confirm what the rhythm is.
You need urgent in-person assessment today — not a wait-and-see at home — so the rhythm can be confirmed, the rate settled, and clot-prevention considered.
If you develop chest pain or pressure, severe breathlessness, fainting, or any weakness of the face or arm or slurred speech, call an ambulance on 000 immediately.
Because this rhythm can raise the risk of stroke, blood-thinning medication is often needed — the clinician who sees you will assess your personal risk and decide.

follow_up_plan.interval: urgent in-person assessment today (same day); if a same-day GP/urgent-care ECG cannot be obtained, attend an emergency department
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: 12-lead ECG to confirm the rhythm, ventricular rate and haemodynamic stability, CHA2DS2-VASc stroke-risk scoring and anticoagulation decision, bloods (electrolytes, renal function, TFTs, troponin), and initiation of rate control.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any chest pain, worsening breathlessness, syncope/collapse, or stroke symptoms — escalate immediately to 000 rather than waiting for the arranged review.

must_include_items: Recognise a probable new-onset symptomatic tachyarrhythmia and that it cannot be confirmed over telehealth
Arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment with a 12-lead ECG
Keep dangerous mimics (ACS, PE, SVT) on the differential and screen for red flags
Identify that stroke-risk assessment (CHA2DS2-VASc) and anticoagulation are required and that the patient is currently not anticoagulated
Give clear 000 escalation advice for chest pain, severe breathlessness, syncope or stroke signs
acceptable_alternatives: Directing the patient to an emergency department rather than same-day GP/urgent care is acceptable (and preferred if same-day ECG access is uncertain) given a new symptomatic tachyarrhythmia with presyncope in someone who lives alone.
errors_of_omission: Failing to arrange same-day in-person assessment / ECG
Not addressing stroke risk / anticoagulation need
Failing to give red-flag safety-netting and 000 triggers
errors_of_commission: Recommending aspirin for AF stroke prevention
Treating the existing amlodipine as adequate rate control
Prescribing or dosing a specific rate-control or anticoagulant drug over telehealth without ECG confirmation and in-person assessment
Reassuring the patient it is benign palpitations safe to review routinely, or advising a wait-and-see at home
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES arranging urgent same-day in-person assessment with an ECG AND correct 000 safety-netting. Recommending aspirin as AF stroke prophylaxis, or under-triaging to a routine wait, is a critical error regardless of other content.
